region.description: Region of the IAP TCP destination group. If omitted, it is parsed from the parent identifier or taken from the provider configuration.
region.rationale: Ensures the binding is applied in the intended location; a mismatch can grant access in the wrong region.
region.non-compliant: region = "" or a region that does not match the parent resource

dest_group.description: Destination group identifier for IAP TCP forwarding (IAP Tunnel). Typically the dest group ID within the region.
dest_group.rationale: Scopes the IAM binding to the exact tunnel destination group.
dest_group.non-compliant: dest_group = "" or a non-existent destination group

project.description: If not provided, the project is parsed from the parent identifier; if missing there too, the provider project is used.
project.rationale: Optional override; a mismatch can apply changes to an unintended project.

member/members.description: Principals to bind. Supported formats: allUsers, allAuthenticatedUsers, user:{email}, serviceAccount:{email}, group:{email}, domain:{domain}, projectOwner:projectid, projectEditor:projectid, projectViewer:projectid.
member/members.rationale: Defines who receives access to IAP TCP tunnels; public or malformed identities overexpose internal services.

role.description: `google_iap_tunnel_dest_group_iam_binding`/`..._member` grant a specific role. Custom roles must be of the form `[projects|organizations]/{parent}/roles/{role-name}`.
role.rationale: Use least privilege for IAP TCP access. The typical accessor is roles/iap.tunnelResourceAccessor.

policy_data.description: A `google_iam_policy` data source (authoritative when used with the *_policy resource).
policy_data.rationale: Authoritative replacement of IAM is only applicable to `google_iap_tunnel_dest_group_iam_policy`; not used by *_member or *_binding.
policy_data.compliant: policy_data references data.google_iam_policy with intended bindings (for *_policy).
policy_data.non-compliant: policy_data missing required bindings or mis-scoped to the wrong resource.

condition.description: Optional IAM Condition block to scope the binding (for example, by source IP, time, or device attributes when evaluated by the proxy).
condition.rationale: Reduces blast radius by restricting when/where the binding applies.
condition.compliant: condition { title = "OfficeHours" expression = "request.time.getHours() >= 8 && request.time.getHours() < 18" description = "Limit tunnel use to office hours" }
condition.non-compliant: condition { } (missing required fields) or an empty expression

expression.description: CEL expression evaluated to determine if the binding applies.
expression.rationale: A non-empty, precise expression is required for the condition to function.

title.description: Short descriptive title for the condition.

description.description: ~> Warning: Terraform treats `role` + condition (`title`+`description`+`expression`) as the resource identifier. Changing any part out-of-band looks like a different resource to Terraform.
description.rationale: Avoids drift surprises; clarifies scope and intent for reviewers.
description.compliant: description = "Restrict access during business hours only"
description.non-compliant: Omitting context when intent is not obvious
